title: User story fields — catalogue, gap analysis, schema recommendation
topic: User story field catalogue validated against Rally docs, naming conventions from R007, schema design
summary: Field catalogue for user story artefacts mapped to Rally equivalents using R007 naming conventions; gap analysis against portfolio item fields; separate user_stories table recommendation with shared custom-field tables extended via entity_kind discriminator.
content:
Section A — User Story Field Catalogue

Every field below is specified for the Vector user story model. Rally equivalents are drawn from the Broadcom Rally user story fields documentation. Where a field name was already established in R007, that name is used verbatim. New names follow R007's snake_case conventions. Types reflect the intended Postgres column or computed nature.

Field nameTypeDescriptionRally equivalentNotes
idUUID (PK)System-assigned unique identifier. Rendered as a human-readable tag at display time (e.g. US-00000001) using key_num and a story prefix. The UUID never changes; the display tag is computed.ID (FormattedID)Same pattern as R007. UUID is the stable identity.
nameTEXT NOT NULLThe human-readable name of the user story. Required. Must be meaningful and unique within its parent scope (uniqueness enforced at application layer).NameIdentical convention to R007 name.
descriptionTEXT (nullable)Rich-text description of the user story — its purpose, scope, and context. Supports markdown or agreed rich-text format.DescriptionIdentical convention to R007 description. Max recommended 64 KB.
acceptance_criteriaTEXT (nullable)Rich-text acceptance criteria defining the conditions that must be met for the story to be considered done. Distinct from description — this is the testable definition of done.No direct Rally equivalent (Rally uses Notes or Description for acceptance criteria)User-story-only field. Store as text (markdown or HTML). Rendered in a dedicated section on the story detail view.
notesTEXT (nullable)Free-text field for additional team decisions, implementation notes, or context that does not belong in description or acceptance_criteria.NotesUser-story-only field at this level; portfolio items use description for this purpose. Max recommended 8 KB.
name_authorUUID FK -> users.id (NOT NULL)The user who created the user story. System-assigned at creation time; never editable.Created By / Submitted ByIdentical convention to R007 name_author. Rally has both Created By and Submitted By (defaults to creator); in Vector these collapse to a single creator field.
name_ownerUUID FK -> users.id (nullable)The user currently responsible for the user story. Defaults to creator at creation; may be changed by padmin or the current owner.OwnerIdentical convention to R007 name_owner. Nullable on user stories (a story may be unassigned in the backlog).
date_createdTIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()Timestamp at which the user story row was inserted. System-generated; never editable.Creation Date (implicit)Identical convention to R007 date_created. Maps to standard created_at column.
date_updatedTIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()Timestamp of the most recent update to the user story row. Maintained by an updated_at trigger.Last UpdatedMaps to standard updated_at column present on all Vector business tables.
date_work_acceptedTIMESTAMPTZ (nullable, computed / materialised)The date on which this story moved into an Accepted / terminal schedule state. NULL if not yet accepted.Accepted DateIn R007 this was computed from child item state history; for user stories it is the story's own state transition to the terminal state. Store as a cached column updated by the state-change handler.
flow_stateUUID FK -> item_type_states.id (nullable)The user story's current position in the flow state board (Kanban column). References the subscription's configured workflow states for user stories.Flow StateIdentical convention to R007 flow_state. Transitions validated against item_type_transition_edges. Flow State is distinct from Schedule State in Rally; Vector uses a single flow state model.
flow_state_change_update_dateTIMESTAMPTZ (nullable)Timestamp of the most recent flow state transition on this story. Enables "time in current state" calculations.State Changed Date (implicit via flow state)Identical convention to R007. Updated atomically with flow_state.
flow_state_change_ownerUUID FK -> users.id (nullable)The user who last changed the flow state of this story. Cached for fast display.No direct Rally equivalentIdentical convention to R007. Authoritative record is in item_state_history.
schedule_stateTEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'defined'The coarse-grained lifecycle state of the user story: one of defined, in_progress, completed, accepted. Distinct from flow_state (Kanban column). Used for backlog filtering and release reporting.Schedule StateUser-story-only field. Rally's Schedule State is a fixed vocabulary (Defined / In-Progress / Completed / Accepted). Store as TEXT with CHECK constraint. Maps to the story's position in the delivery lifecycle, independent of Kanban column.
blockedbooleanIndicates the user story is currently blocked. Can be set manually by the owner or padmin.Blocked / StatusIdentical convention to R007 blocked. Rally combines Blocked boolean and Status (Ready/Blocked) into the Status field; Vector collapses these.
blocked_reasonTEXT (nullable)Free-text explanation of why the story is blocked. Required when blocked = true is set manually.Blocked ReasonIdentical convention to R007 blocked_reason. Conditional display: only shown when blocked = true.
readyboolean NOT NULL DEFAULT falseIndicates the story is ready to be pulled into the next sprint or stage. A grooming signal set by the team or padmin.ReadyUser-story-only field. Rally's Ready flag is a common grooming practice. Store as boolean. Mutually exclusive with blocked at the application layer (a ready story should not simultaneously be blocked).
expediteboolean NOT NULL DEFAULT falseMarks the story as expedited — it should be pulled ahead of normal priority ordering. Triggers visual highlighting on boards.ExpediteUser-story-only field. A swimlane/WIP override signal used in Kanban teams. Store as boolean.
hierarchy_parentUUID FK -> portfolio_items.id (nullable)Reference to the parent portfolio item (feature or lowest-level portfolio item) that this user story belongs to. NULL for stories in the flat backlog with no portfolio parent.Feature / Parent and HierarchyIdentical convention to R007 hierarchy_parent, but the FK target is a portfolio item (feature level), not another user story. Rally calls this the "Feature" field (lowest-level portfolio item parent) and also exposes a generic Parent and Hierarchy field. Stories cannot parent other stories in Vector (tasks are the child level).
sprintUUID FK -> iterations.id (nullable)The sprint or iteration to which this user story is assigned. NULL for unscheduled backlog stories.IterationUser-story-only field. References a future iterations table (not yet built). FK nullable: stories may exist outside any iteration.
releaseUUID FK -> releases.id (nullable)The release to which this user story is assigned. NULL if not yet targeted to a release.ReleaseUser-story-only field. References a future releases table. FK nullable.
estimate_pointsNUMERIC(6,1) (nullable)Story point estimate for this user story — the primary planning unit for sprint capacity. Represents effort or complexity relative to other stories.Plan EstimateUser-story-only field. Rally uses integer story points; Vector uses NUMERIC(6,1) to allow half-point estimates. NULL = unestimated.
estimate_hoursNUMERIC(8,2) (nullable)Total task-level hour estimate for this story — the sum of estimated hours across all child tasks. May be entered directly or rolled up from child tasks.Task Estimate (rollup)User-story-only field. Rally's Task Rollup aggregates task estimate across children. In Vector, this may be stored directly (padmin enters at story level) or computed from child tasks.
estimate_remainingNUMERIC(8,2) (nullable)Remaining hours of effort to complete all child tasks on this story. Updated as tasks are completed or estimates revised.To Do (rollup)User-story-only field. Rally's To Do field is per-task; the story-level view aggregates. In Vector, store at story level as a materialised rollup from child tasks.
rankTEXT NOT NULLLexoRank ordering key used to sort this story within its backlog or board column. Enables drag-and-drop reordering without renumbering all rows.No direct Rally equivalent (Rally uses drag-and-drop rank internally)User-story-only field at this level (portfolio items use their own rank). LexoRank: a string-based ordering system where ranks are spaced to allow efficient mid-point insertion. A pending LexoRank story covers the implementation.
count_child_tasksINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total count of child tasks directly parented to this user story. Computed at reporting time or incrementally.Task Status (count component)User-story-only field. Analogous to R007 count_child_user_stories but for tasks.
count_child_defectsINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total count of defect-type items directly linked to this user story.Defects (count component)Shared name with R007 count_child_defects — same concept, same name, applied at the story level.
count_child_test_casesINTEGER (computed / materialised)Total count of test cases linked to this user story.Test Cases (count component)User-story-only field. Rally surfaces test cases as a linked collection; Vector stores the count as a computed field.
done_by_task_countNUMERIC(5,2) (computed)Percentage of child tasks in Accepted/terminal state vs. total child tasks. Computed as (accepted_task_count / total_task_count) * 100. NULL if no child tasks exist.Task Rollup (percentage component)Analogous to R007 done_by_story_count but for tasks. Computed, not stored.
test_case_statusTEXT (computed / materialised)Rollup status of linked test case results: one of none, passed, failed, mixed. Computed from the most recent execution results of all linked test cases.Test Case Status / Last VerdictUser-story-only field. Rally exposes Last Verdict (per test case) and Test Case Status (rollup). Vector stores the rolled-up summary value.
defect_statusTEXT (computed / materialised)Rolled-up status of linked defects: one of none, open, fixed, mixed. Computed from the states of all child defects.Defect StatusUser-story-only field. A quick-glance indicator without requiring drill-down into linked defects.
affects_docboolean NOT NULL DEFAULT falseFlags that this story has documentation impact — i.e., product documentation must be updated as part of delivery. Used by doc teams to filter stories requiring doc work.Affects DocUser-story-only field. Simple boolean flag. May drive a documentation backlog view or filter.
risk_scoreNUMERIC(5,2) (nullable)Overall delivery risk assessment score for this user story. May be set manually or derived from linked risk records.Risks (score component)Identical convention to R007 risk_score. Rally exposes a Risks field (collection of associated risks); Vector stores a scalar score at the story level. Full risk detail lives in a linked risks table.
risk_impactTEXT or INTEGER (nullable)The assessed impact level if the primary risk materialises: Low / Medium / High / Critical.No direct Rally equivalentIdentical convention to R007 risk_impact. Optional at story level; more commonly set on portfolio items.
risk_probabilityTEXT or INTEGER (nullable)The assessed probability that the primary risk will occur.No direct Rally equivalentIdentical convention to R007 risk_probability.
lidentifier_colourTEXT (nullable)Hex colour code for the visual label badge on this story in board and list views.No direct Rally equivalentIdentical convention to R007 lidentifier_colour.
lidentifier_labelsTEXT[] (nullable)Array of short display labels attached to the story badge.Tags (partial)Identical convention to R007 lidentifier_labels.
lidentifier_tagsTEXT[] (nullable)Array of metadata tags for filtering, search, and reporting.TagsIdentical convention to R007 lidentifier_tags.
archivedboolean (derived from archived_at IS NOT NULL)Marks the story as archived. Archived stories are excluded from live queries but retained for audit.No direct Rally equivalentIdentical convention to R007 archived. Stored as archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
discussions— (separate system)User stories participate in the same threaded discussion system as portfolio items. One thread per story, using entity_kind = 'user_story'.DiscussionsIdentical convention to R007 discussions. The discussion tables from R007 Section D apply directly via the entity_kind discriminator.
attachments— (separate system)Files attached to this user story. Stored as pointers (storage key + metadata) in a story attachments table, not binary data in Postgres.AttachmentsUser-story-level field. Rally supports up to 50 MB per file. Vector uses the same storage-pointer pattern as discussion_attachments. A future story_attachments table or a generalised entity_attachments table covers this.

Section B — Gap Analysis vs R007

Fields shared with portfolio items (same column, same table or joined)

These fields apply identically to both portfolio items and user stories. Where a unified table approach is used, they are the same column. Where separate tables are used, they share the same name and semantics.

Field nameShared behaviour
idUUID PK pattern is identical; display tag prefix differs (portfolio type tag vs. US-).
nameTEXT NOT NULL; same validation rules.
descriptionRich text; same storage and rendering approach.
name_authorCreator FK; system-assigned; never editable. Same semantics.
name_ownerResponsible user FK; same assignment and reassignment rules.
date_createdStandard created_at column; same trigger pattern.
blockedBoolean blocker flag; same display and validation rules.
blocked_reasonConditional free text; same length and display rules.
flow_stateFK to item_type_states; same transition validation system. States may differ per item type.
flow_state_change_update_dateCached last-changed timestamp; same update pattern.
flow_state_change_ownerCached last-changer FK; same denorm pattern.
risk_scoreScalar risk score; same column type and derivation options.
risk_impactImpact category; same CHECK vocabulary.
risk_probabilityProbability category; same CHECK vocabulary.
lidentifier_colourBadge colour hex; same CHECK constraint.
lidentifier_labelsBadge labels array; same storage.
lidentifier_tagsMetadata tags array; same storage.
archivedSoft-archive via archived_at; same invariant (no hard deletes).
count_child_defectsCount of linked defects; same computed/materialised pattern.
discussionsSame discussion tables; entity_kind discriminator distinguishes portfolio_item from user_story.

Fields that are user-story-only (not applicable to portfolio items)

Field nameReason it is story-only
acceptance_criteriaPortfolio items use description for scope; acceptance criteria is an execution-layer concept.
notesPortfolio items do not have a separate notes field; description covers this at that level.
schedule_statePortfolio items use flow_state exclusively; schedule state is a user story delivery-lifecycle concept from Rally.
readyGrooming signal specific to execution-layer backlog refinement.
expediteKanban expedite swimlane concept applies at story/task level only.
sprintSprints/iterations are an execution-layer scheduling construct; portfolio items are scheduled to releases and roadmap windows.
releaseDirect release assignment at story level; portfolio items may associate with releases via rollup but do not hold a direct FK.
estimate_pointsStory points are an execution-layer estimation unit; portfolio items use estimate_initial (T-shirt) and estimate_updated (refined points at portfolio level).
estimate_hoursTask-hour estimates are story/task-level concepts.
estimate_remainingRemaining hours are a sprint-execution concept.
rankLexoRank within backlog — a story-level ordering field; portfolio items have their own ordering within hierarchy levels.
count_child_tasksTasks are children of stories, not of portfolio items.
count_child_test_casesTest cases are linked at story level; portfolio items surface test case counts via rollup only.
done_by_task_countTask-completion percentage is a story-level metric.
test_case_statusTest execution rollup is story-level.
defect_statusDefect rollup status is story-level; portfolio items use count_rollup_defect (integer) not a status summary.
affects_docDocumentation impact flag is a story-level delivery concern.
attachmentsFile attachments at story level; portfolio items do not currently have a direct attachments model.
date_work_accepted (story semantics)At story level this is the story's own accepted-date (direct state transition), not a rollup from children as in R007.

R007 fields explicitly NOT applicable to user stories

R007 fieldWhy it does not apply to user stories
strategic_investment_groupInvestment bucket classification is a portfolio-level strategic concept; stories inherit investment context from their parent portfolio item.
strategic_investment_weightSame: portfolio-level weighting concept.
strategic_item_typeType-of-strategic-work classification is portfolio-level.
value_stream_identifierValue stream association is portfolio-level; stories inherit via parent.
count_child_user_storiesPortfolio items count their child stories; stories do not have child stories (tasks are the child level).
count_rollup_estimationCross-hierarchy story-point rollup is a portfolio-level metric; at story level, estimate_points is the direct value.
count_rollup_defectAccepted-defect rollup across a portfolio hierarchy subtree does not apply at story level.
count_rollup_defectsTotal-defect rollup across hierarchy does not apply at story level.
count_rollup_risksRisk count rollup across hierarchy subtree is portfolio-level only.
count_dependantsDependency tracking between portfolio items is portfolio-level; story-level dependencies are a separate future concern.
estimate_initialT-shirt sizing is a portfolio-level intake concept; stories use story points (estimate_points).
estimate_updatedRefined portfolio-level estimate; stories use estimate_points directly.
done_by_story_countPortfolio-level metric (% of child stories accepted); replaced at story level by done_by_task_count.
date_work_planned_startPortfolio roadmap dates; stories use iteration and release assignments for scheduling.
date_work_planned_finishPortfolio roadmap dates; same reasoning.
date_work_startedPortfolio-level rollup from first child going in-progress; at story level, flow_state transitions capture this.
lidentifier_typePortfolio item type (Feature / Initiative / Theme level discriminator); user stories have a fixed type and do not use the portfolio_item_types catalogue.
hierarchy_childPortfolio items list their child portfolio items; user stories' children are tasks (different relationship).

Section C — Schema Recommendation

Recommendation: separate user_stories table

User stories should have their own user_stories table rather than sharing the portfolio_items table via a type discriminator. The rationale:

  • Field divergence is high. User stories have 18+ fields that do not exist on portfolio items (acceptance_criteria, schedule_state, ready, expedite, iteration, release, estimate_points, estimate_hours, estimate_remaining, rank, count_child_tasks, count_child_test_cases, done_by_task_count, test_case_status, defect_status, affects_doc, notes, attachments). A shared table would have many nullable columns that are structurally irrelevant to one side — a violation of first normal form semantics and a maintenance burden.
  • Query patterns diverge. Portfolio item queries are hierarchical (recursive CTEs, parent-child walks, rollup aggregations). User story queries are backlog/sprint oriented (iteration filter, rank order, schedule state, acceptance criteria search). Mixing these in one table makes index design harder and query plans less predictable.
  • Hierarchy relationship is parent-child, not peer. User stories are children of portfolio items (via hierarchy_parent FK). They do not share a level in the hierarchy — they are a different entity class at a different layer. A type discriminator in a shared table is appropriate when the entities are peers (same level, same shape). Here they are not.
  • Shared fields are addressable by convention. The 18 shared fields (name, description, blocked, flow_state, etc.) follow identical naming and semantics across both tables. Application code can treat these as a shared interface without requiring a single physical table.
  • Future task table follows the same pattern. Tasks will be children of user stories, also with their own table. The consistent pattern is: one table per execution layer (portfolio_items, user_stories, tasks).

CREATE TABLE sketch (key columns only)

CREATE TABLE user_stories (
    -- Identity
    id                          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id             UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    workspace_id                UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES workspace(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    key_num                     BIGINT      NOT NULL GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY,

    -- Core content
    name                        TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (length(trim(name)) > 0),
    description                 TEXT,
    acceptance_criteria         TEXT,
    notes                       TEXT,

    -- Ownership
    name_author                 UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    name_owner                  UUID        REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    -- Hierarchy
    hierarchy_parent            UUID        REFERENCES portfolio_items(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    -- Scheduling
    iteration                   UUID        REFERENCES iterations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    release                     UUID        REFERENCES releases(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,

    -- Flow and schedule state
    flow_state                  UUID        REFERENCES item_type_states(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    flow_state_change_update_date TIMESTAMPTZ,
    flow_state_change_owner     UUID        REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    schedule_state              TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'defined'
                                    CHECK (schedule_state IN ('defined', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'accepted')),
    date_work_accepted          TIMESTAMPTZ,

    -- Estimation
    estimate_points             NUMERIC(6,1),
    estimate_hours              NUMERIC(8,2),
    estimate_remaining          NUMERIC(8,2),

    -- Flags
    blocked                     BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    blocked_reason              TEXT,
    ready                       BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    expedite                    BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    affects_doc                 BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,

    -- Risk
    risk_score                  NUMERIC(5,2),
    risk_impact                 TEXT        CHECK (risk_impact IN ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical')),
    risk_probability            TEXT        CHECK (risk_probability IN ('low', 'medium', 'high')),

    -- Visual identifier
    lidentifier_colour          TEXT        CHECK (lidentifier_colour ~* '^#[0-9a-f]{6}$'),
    lidentifier_labels          TEXT[],
    lidentifier_tags            TEXT[],

    -- Ordering
    rank                        TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT '0|hzzzzz:',

    -- Computed rollups (materialised or computed on read)
    count_child_tasks           INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    count_child_defects         INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    count_child_test_cases      INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    test_case_status            TEXT        CHECK (test_case_status IN ('none', 'passed', 'failed', 'mixed')),
    defect_status               TEXT        CHECK (defect_status IN ('none', 'open', 'fixed', 'mixed')),

    -- Soft archive
    archived_at                 TIMESTAMPTZ,

    -- Audit
    created_at                  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at                  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_us_subscription       ON user_stories(subscription_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_us_workspace          ON user_stories(workspace_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_us_hierarchy_parent   ON user_stories(hierarchy_parent) WHERE hierarchy_parent IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_iteration          ON user_stories(iteration) WHERE iteration IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_release            ON user_stories(release) WHERE release IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_flow_state         ON user_stories(flow_state) WHERE archived_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_schedule_state     ON user_stories(schedule_state) WHERE archived_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_owner              ON user_stories(name_owner) WHERE archived_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_us_rank               ON user_stories(hierarchy_parent, rank) WHERE archived_at IS NULL;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_us_updated_at
    BEFORE UPDATE ON user_stories
    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();

Section D — Custom Fields

Recommendation: extend the same tables with an entity_kind discriminator

The custom field design from R007 (portfolio_item_field_definitions + portfolio_item_field_values) should be extended to user stories using the same tables with an entity_kind discriminator column added to both tables. Separate tables are not needed.

Rationale

  • The field type vocabulary is identical. The nine supported types (text, textarea, number, boolean, date, select, multiselect, user, url) apply equally to portfolio items and user stories. No new types are needed.
  • The value storage design is identical. The hybrid EAV approach (one row per item/field, typed nullable columns) works for user stories without modification.
  • The scope model extends naturally. Field definitions are currently scoped to (workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id). For user stories, the scope is (workspace_id, entity_kind = 'user_story') — no portfolio item type discriminator is needed since user stories are a single type. Adding entity_kind to the definitions table achieves this cleanly.
  • The discussion system already uses this pattern. R007's discussion_threads table uses entity_kind IN ('portfolio_item', 'execution_item'). Extending to user_story is the same operation.
  • Operational simplicity. One set of tables for all custom field definitions and values means one migration path, one admin UI, one API surface. Separate tables would duplicate all of this.

Migration plan for existing tables

The tables defined in R007 need two changes to support user stories:

  1. Rename portfolio_item_field_definitions to item_field_definitions (or keep the name and add entity_kind). The rename is cleaner long-term.
  2. Add entity_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'portfolio_item' CHECK (entity_kind IN ('portfolio_item', 'user_story', 'task')) to the definitions table.
  3. Add entity_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'portfolio_item' to the values table, and update the FK reference from portfolio_item_id to a generic entity_id UUID NOT NULL (polymorphic, not a hard FK — same pattern as discussion_threads.entity_id).
  4. Update the UNIQUE constraint on field definitions: from (workspace_id, portfolio_item_type_id, custom_field_label) to (workspace_id, entity_kind, portfolio_item_type_id, custom_field_label) — where portfolio_item_type_id is NULL for user_story and task definitions.

Revised table sketch (key changes only)

-- Extended field definitions table (rename from portfolio_item_field_definitions)
CREATE TABLE item_field_definitions (
    id                      UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id         UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    workspace_id            UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES workspace(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    -- Discriminator: which entity kind this definition applies to
    entity_kind             TEXT        NOT NULL
                                CHECK (entity_kind IN ('portfolio_item', 'user_story', 'task')),

    -- For portfolio_item definitions: which item type; NULL for user_story and task
    portfolio_item_type_id  UUID        REFERENCES portfolio_item_types(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    custom_field_label      TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (length(trim(custom_field_label)) > 0),
    custom_field_description TEXT,
    custom_field_type       TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (custom_field_type IN (
                                'text', 'textarea', 'number', 'boolean',
                                'date', 'select', 'multiselect', 'user', 'url'
                            )),
    custom_field_required   BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    custom_field_position   INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    custom_field_creator    UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    archived_at             TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at              TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT ifd_label_unique UNIQUE (workspace_id, entity_kind, portfolio_item_type_id, custom_field_label)
);

-- Extended field values table (rename from portfolio_item_field_values)
CREATE TABLE item_field_values (
    id                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    -- Polymorphic entity reference (no hard FK — enforced at application layer)
    entity_kind         TEXT        NOT NULL
                            CHECK (entity_kind IN ('portfolio_item', 'user_story', 'task')),
    entity_id           UUID        NOT NULL,

    field_definition_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES item_field_definitions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,

    value_text          TEXT,
    value_number        NUMERIC(18, 4),
    value_boolean       BOOLEAN,
    value_date          DATE,
    value_jsonb         JSONB,

    set_by              UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT ifv_entity_field_unique UNIQUE (entity_kind, entity_id, field_definition_id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_ifv_subscription ON item_field_values(subscription_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ifv_entity       ON item_field_values(entity_kind, entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ifv_definition   ON item_field_values(field_definition_id);

Polymorphic FK note

The entity_id column in item_field_values is a polymorphic reference — it points to a row in either portfolio_items, user_stories, or a future tasks table depending on entity_kind. As per the Vector polymorphic FK pattern documented in docs/c_polymorphic_writes.md, application-layer enforcement replaces a DB-level FK constraint. The cleanup registry must be updated to include item_field_values as a dependent of user_stories (and later tasks).
